skill.name: network-and-relationship-manager
skill.description: Never forget a person again — search your entire professional network, recall exactly what you discussed, and always know who you know at any company. Powered by Rarefriend personal CRM.
skill.summary: Search contacts, log meeting notes, schedule events, and manage your professional network via the Rarefriend MCP integration.
skill.author: Whitefield-Labs

security_audit.summary: The skill is a pure Markdown documentation file describing how to use the Rarefriend MCP server. It contains no executable code. The 169 'external_commands' findings are false positives — they are markdown code block backticks, not shell execution. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives — they reference OAuth client secrets stored in env vars (standard practice). The 'Hidden file in home directory' findings are about legitimate config paths (~/.cursor/mcp.json, ~/.config/Claude/). The 'Hardcoded URL' findings are the rarefriend.com documentation link. The 'System reconnaissance' findings are about setup instructions for the MCP integration. No malicious intent found.

low_findings.title: OAuth credentials required in environment variables
low_findings.description: The skill requires RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_ID and RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables for MCP authentication. This is standard OAuth2 client credentials flow, not a security concern, but users should ensure secrets are not committed to version control.

low_findings.title: Config files written to user home directory
low_findings.description: Setup instructions direct users to edit configuration files in ~/.cursor/mcp.json and ~/.config/Claude/. These are standard application config paths and the instructions are transparent to the user.

content.user_title: Manage Your Professional Network with AI
content.value_statement: Professionals lose track of important contacts and conversation context. This skill connects Claude and Codex to your Rarefriend CRM so you can search contacts, log meeting notes, and schedule events by voice or text.
actual_capabilities: Search contacts by name, company, role, email, or tag using natural language queries
Create, update, and delete contacts with full profile fields including notes and tags
Log meeting notes and call summaries against specific contacts with markdown support
Search full-text across all notes to recall what you discussed with someone
Schedule Google Calendar and Microsoft Outlook events with attendee resolution from contacts
Bulk import up to 50 contacts at a time from text, CSV, or spreadsheet data
Connect Gmail, Google Contacts, Google Calendar, and Microsoft integrations via OAuth links
limitations: Requires the Rarefriend MCP server (@rarefriend-ai/mcp) to be installed and configured before use
LinkedIn sync requires the separate Rarefriend Chrome extension — not available through the MCP
Bulk import is capped at 50 contacts per call; larger batches must be split sequentially
Plan-based contact quotas apply; quota_exceeded responses require upgrading at rarefriend.com

use_cases.title: Recall context before a meeting
use_cases.description: Ask 'What do I know about Sarah Chen?' to retrieve all notes, tags, and interaction history before walking into a meeting.

use_cases.title: Log notes after a call
use_cases.description: After a discovery call, ask the assistant to create a note on the contact with a summary of what was discussed.

use_cases.title: Find warm introductions at a target company
use_cases.description: Ask 'Who do I know at Stripe?' to search your network and identify potential warm intro paths for a deal.

prompt_templates.title: Search for a contact
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my Rarefriend contacts for people who work at Anthropic. Show their name, role, and how I know them.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quickly find existing connections at a company

prompt_templates.title: Log a meeting note
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a note on John Smith's contact: 'Discussed Q3 partnership expansion. They are interested in the enterprise tier. Follow up next Tuesday with a proposal.'
prompt_templates.scenario: Capture meeting context immediately after a call

prompt_templates.title: Schedule with availability check
prompt_templates.prompt: Find a 30-minute slot next week between 10am and 4pm that works for me and [email protected]. Then create a Google Calendar event called 'Product demo' with that time slot.

prompt_templates.title: Bulk import from a list
prompt_templates.prompt: Import these contacts into Rarefriend: 1) Alice Wong, VP Engineering at Acme Corp, [email protected] 2) Bob Lee, Designer at Beta Inc, [email protected]. Use bulk_create_contacts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Onboard a batch of new connections from an event

output_examples.input: Who do I know at Stripe?
output_examples.output: You have 3 connections at Stripe: 1) Sarah Chen — VP Product (Google Contacts). You last spoke in March 2025 about API rate limits. 2) Mike Rodriguez — Engineering Manager (LinkedIn). Connected at a conference in 2024. 3) Priya Patel — Recruiter (Added manually). Tag: recruiter.

output_examples.input: Add a note to my contact David Kim: 'Met at SaaStr 2026. Interested in our API product. Send him the developer docs link this week.'
output_examples.output: Note created on David Kim's contact. The note is pinned so it will appear in search results for 'SaaStr' and 'API'. You can find David in your contacts with the tag 'conference-2026' if you want to add that.

best_practices: Always call list_connected_integrations first to check MCP and integration status before performing any CRM action
Normalize tags to lowercase kebab-case (e.g., 'warm-intro' not 'Warm Intro') for consistent filtering
Pin critical context notes so they surface in search results for time-sensitive relationships
anti_patterns: Do not call get_contact for every contact in a search result — search first, then fetch only the specific person asked about
Do not ask the user to paste OAuth credentials into the chat — use the connect_integration flow which returns a secure link
Do not attempt bulk imports larger than 50 contacts in a single call — split into sequential batches and respect rate limits

faq.question: What is Rarefriend?
faq.answer: Rarefriend is a personal CRM that stores your professional contacts from Google, LinkedIn, Microsoft, and your phone in one searchable place with notes, tags, and relationship history.

faq.question: Do I need to pay for Rarefriend to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a Rarefriend account. Free and paid tiers exist. Some features like bulk import are subject to plan-based contact quotas.

faq.question: How do I get the OAuth credentials?
faq.answer: Sign in at rarefriend.com, go to Settings then Integrations then MCP, and copy your Client ID and Client Secret. Then configure them as environment variables for the MCP server.

faq.question: Can this skill access my Google Contacts without permission?
faq.answer: No. You must explicitly connect Google or Microsoft integrations through the connect_integration flow, which opens a secure OAuth link in your browser.

faq.question: Why are there 169 security findings on this skill?
faq.answer: The static scanner flags every markdown backtick as a potential shell command. This skill is pure documentation with no executable code. All findings are false positives.

faq.question: What AI tools does this skill work with?
faq.answer: It works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, as well as any Agent Skills-compatible client including Cursor, OpenClaw, and Gemini CLI.
